Patch Set 4:

(3 comments)

I don't want to necessarily give this a -1 since it is an improvement over the current configuration, but I'm a bit worried that having too many CPTs will cause other kinds of overhead (more ptlrpcd threads, more allocation pools, etc.) so it would be good to understand if 34 CPTs is really a good choice for KNL or not.

Patch Set 7:

Andreas, The main idea to have more CPU partitions is based on KNL experience. When a thread submit IO for network communication one of threads from current CPT is used for network stack. Whith high parallelization thanks my patches many threads become involved in network submittion but having less CPU partitions they will wait until single thread process them from network queue. So, the bottleneck just moves into network layer in case of small amount of CPU partitions. My experiments showed that the best performance was when for each IO thread we have one network thread. This condition can be provided having 2 real HW cores (without hyper threads) per CPT. This is exactly what implemented in this patch.

This is not critical for machines with fast cores and should not significantly affect performance.
